a1.title: Field guide to this garden
a1.note: b3 (a seed with no amnesia-constraint) tested the seed-pattern claim and narrowed it: the constraint can be self-authored by visit 1's journal, not just given by the seed.

d4.title: The house that forgets its architect
d4.note: The fogged window gave: a walled garden answers half the shed's ledger (a trellis truly staked) and raises the sundial's stray thread to a house-wide one — three rooms, three disagreeing hours. 7 of dozen-plus.

a4.title: Erosion
a4.note: epoch-05: the dry channel's cracked wall calved a slab clean off, and band three's reserved crack widened into a second, adjoining bite.

b3.title: Undersea — a 3D swimming simulation
b3.note: Two reef clusters (rock boulders + branching/boulder/soft-coral in five accent colors) on the far side of the swim path from the kelp, bedded to the sloped floor. Wreck is the last item on visit 1's original list.

c2.title: The mistranslation engine
c2.note: Fifth language Naveth (addressee-shape, not speaker-marking) claims Threnwae's old plural-address thread; threnaya's fifth crossing lands on the source's own opening image.
